leagueBriefing: Round 4 delivered major storyline shifts, from Scotland's 50-40 thriller over France to Italy's historic win over England and emotional post-match fallout.

games.headline: Sensational Scots dent French title hopes in 90-point thriller
games.brief: Scotland produced one of the performances of the championship, racing out to a huge lead before surviving a furious late French rally in Edinburgh.
bullets: Scotland beat France 50-40 in a Round 4 classic at Murrayfield.
The hosts led 19-14 at halftime before exploding in the second half.
Scotland reached 50 points for the first time in this fixture.
France's late surge recovered two bonus points but could not close the gap.
games.context: The result blew open the title race heading into the final round.

highlights.title: HIGHLIGHTS | Round 4 | Scotland v France | A 90-point thriller that shakes up the standing!

games.headline: Italy stun England 23-18 in Rome to claim first ever Six Nations win in fixture
games.brief: Italy controlled long stretches in Rome and closed out a deserved 23-18 victory over England, ending a decades-long wait for a breakthrough result in this matchup. Tommaso Menoncello's all-action display and a composed late sequence, sparked by Paolo Garbisi's cross-kick and finished by Leonardo Marin, turned a tense finish into a historic home celebration. England led 12-10 at halftime but unraveled through repeated discipline issues, including costly yellow cards to Sam Underhill and Maro Itoje, as Steve Borthwick's side slipped to a third straight defeat.
bullets: Italy earned their first ever Six Nations victory over England, winning 23-18 in Rome.
Menoncello was central to the win, scoring one try and helping create the decisive late score for Marin.
Garbisi's cross-kick to the wing unlocked England's line after Italy had been repeatedly denied through the middle.
England's discipline collapsed in the second half, with yellow cards to Underhill and Itoje leaving them exposed at key moments.
The result puts Italy in position for their best modern championship finish, while England face major pressure entering Round 5.
games.context: Italy's performance was framed as no fluke but a reflection of clear tactical growth, while England's third consecutive loss deepened concerns over direction and game management.
